title: aetheolaena longepenicillata
intro: Aetheolaena longepenicillata, also known as the Katsura tree or Long-pecillata, is a species of plant in the family Compositae, native to the Pacific Islands. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, including the presence of sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been used to treat various health conditions. Aetheolaena longepenicillata has been traditionally used to treat fever, headache, and inflammation due to its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as pain relief, reduced inflammation, and immune system support. Herbal preparations of Aetheolaena longepenicillata include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and topical creams for various health applications.
